description: Verify employee_id column is INT64 type
query: SELECT data_type FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name = 'employee_id'

query: SELECT data_type FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name = 'commission_pct'

query: SELECT data_type FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name = 'hire_date'

description: Verify created_date column is TIMESTAMP type
query: SELECT data_type FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name = 'created_date'

description: Verify updated_date column is TIMESTAMP type
query: SELECT data_type FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name = 'updated_date'

description: Verify all VARCHAR2 source columns mapped to STRING
query: SELECT column_name, data_type FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name IN ('first_name','last_name','email','phone_number','job_id','status')

test_name: Validate INT64 columns for ID fields
description: Verify manager_id and department_id are INT64
query: SELECT column_name, data_type FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name IN ('manager_id','department_id')

description: Verify no unexpected columns exist in stg_employees
query: SELECT column_name FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name NOT IN ('employee_id','first_name','last_name','email','phone_number','hire_date','job_id','salary','commission_pct','manager_id','department_id','status','created_date','updated_date')

description: Verify no null values in primary key column
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE employee_id IS NULL

description: Verify no duplicate values in primary key
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS dup_count FROM (SELECT employee_id FROM `stg_employees` GROUP BY employee_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1)

description: Verify no null values in email column
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE email IS NULL

description: Verify salary column has no negative values
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS neg_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE salary < 0

description: Verify commission_pct values between 0 and 1
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS invalid_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE commission_pct IS NOT NULL AND (commission_pct < 0 OR commission_pct > 1)

description: Verify no null values in hire_date column
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE hire_date IS NULL

description: Verify hire_date values are not in future
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS future_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE hire_date > CURRENT_DATE()

description: Verify target row count equals source row count
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS target_row_count FROM `stg_employees`
expected_result: target_row_count equals MySQL EMPLOYEES source row count

description: Verify no null values in partition column
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE created_date IS NULL

description: Verify no null values in job_id column
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE job_id IS NULL

test_name: Validate first_name and last_name not null
description: Verify no null values in name columns
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE first_name IS NULL OR last_name IS NULL

description: Verify no null values in department_id column
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE department_id IS NULL

description: Verify employee_id CAST produces no null from valid source
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cast_fail_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE SAFE_CAST(CAST(employee_id AS STRING) AS INT64) IS NULL AND employee_id IS NOT NULL

description: Verify stg_employees is partitioned by created_date
query: SELECT partition_column FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees'

description: Verify partition column created_date is TIMESTAMP
query: SELECT data_type FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND column_name = 'created_date'

description: Verify query uses partition filter on created_date
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE created_date >= TIMESTAMP('2024-01-01') AND created_date < TIMESTAMP('2024-02-01')
expected_result: Query executes with partition pruning, no full table scan
linked_ac: AC: Pipeline handles 10 GB data volume

test_name: Validate cluster columns employee_id and department_id
description: Verify table is clustered by employee_id and department_id
query: SELECT clustering_columns FROM `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES` WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees'

test_name: Validate no null values in partition column
description: Verify created_date has no nulls to avoid null partition
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_partition_count FROM `stg_employees` WHERE created_date IS NULL

description: Verify number of partitions is within expected range
query: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT DATE(created_date)) AS partition_count FROM `stg_employees`
expected_result: partition_count > 0 and within expected date range

description: Verify no single partition holds all records
query: SELECT DATE(created_date) AS partition_date, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM `stg_employees` GROUP BY partition_date ORDER BY row_count DESC LIMIT 1
expected_result: Top partition row_count < total row count of stg_employees
